title: Balance transfer
description: Practice understanding of purpose of introductory APR, balance transfer fee, and how it impacts your debt payoff plan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Banks offer a $0\%$ introductory balance transfer APR to attract new customers, but they use different ways to make money even with this offer.

**How do banks make money with a $0\%$ APR?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: they increase your spending limit to get you to spend more
they charge you a fee on the balance you transfer
they give special rewards for transferring large balances
they charge the other bank to take over the balance

assessment_items.question_content:
Banks offer $0\%$ APR to attract customers, but they have ways to earn money even with these offers.

**In which scenario does a bank most likely profit from a $0\%$ APR offer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You pay off your balance before the introductory period ends.
You make a large payment to pay off the balance.
You apply for a $0\%$ APR, but do not transfer the balance.
You still owe a balance after the introductory period ends.

assessment_items.question_content:
Banks use $0\%$ APR offers to attract customers but can still make money depending on how you use the card.

**In which scenario can a bank profit from a 0% APR offer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You only use the card for balance transfers.
You do not use the card for any purchases.
You make new purchases that don’t have the $0\%$ APR.
You pay off your entire balance before making purchases.

assessment_items.question_content:
You currently have a $\$4{,}000$ balance on a credit card that has a $29\%$ APR. In the mail you receive a credit card offer that has the following terms:

 | Intro APR | Intro period | Balance transfer fee | Regular APR 
:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
**Ruby card** | $0\%$ | $9$ months | $4\%$ | $22\%$ 

You would like to transfer your entire credit card balance to the Ruby card.

**What will the initial balance on the Ruby card be after the transfer?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Since you will be transferring the entire balance to the Ruby card, a *balance transfer fee* will apply to this transfer.

The balance transfer fee is $4\%$ of the amount you are transferring.

$0.04 \times \$4{,}000 = \$160$

What you now owe to the Ruby card company is your old balance and the transfer fee, which they simply add to your balance.

$\begin{align} \text{Initial balance} &= \text{Old balance} + \text{transfer fee}
\\\\
&=\$4{,}000 + \$160
\\\\
&=\$4{,}160
\end{align}$
The initial balance on the Ruby card after the transfer is $\$$ $\boxed{4{,}160}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You currently have a $\$3{,}000$ balance on a credit card that has a $29\%$ APR. In the mail you receive a credit card offer that has the following terms:

 | Intro APR | Intro period | Balance transfer fee | Regular APR 
:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
**Gold card** | $0\%$ | $9$ months | $3\%$ | $22\%$ 

You would like to transfer your entire credit card balance to the Gold card.

**What will the initial balance on the Gold card be after the transfer?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Since you will be transferring the entire balance to the Gold card, a *balance transfer fee* will apply to this transfer.

The balance transfer fee is $3\%$ of the amount you are transferring.

$0.03 \times \$3{,}000 = \$90$

What you now owe to the Gold card company is your old balance and the transfer fee, which they simply add to your balance.

$\begin{align} \text{Initial balance} &= \text{Old balance} + \text{transfer fee}
\\\\
&=\$3{,}000 + \$90
\\\\
&=\$3{,}090
\end{align}$
The initial balance on the Gold card after the transfer is $\$$ $\boxed{3{,}090}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You currently have a $\$6{,}000$ balance on a credit card that has a $29\%$ APR. In the mail you receive a credit card offer that has the following terms:

 | Intro APR | Intro period | Balance transfer fee | Regular APR 
:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
**Emerald card** | $0\%$ | $9$ months | $2\%$ | $22\%$ 

You would like to transfer your entire credit card balance to the Emerald card.

**What will the initial balance on the Emerald card be after the transfer?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Since you will be transferring the entire balance to the Emerald card, a *balance transfer fee* will apply to this transfer.

The balance transfer fee is $2\%$ of the amount you are transferring.

$0.02 \times \$6{,}000 = \$120$

What you now owe to the Emerald card company is your old balance and the transfer fee, which they simply add to your balance.

$\begin{align} \text{Initial balance} &= \text{Old balance} + \text{transfer fee}
\\\\
&=\$6{,}000 + \$120
\\\\
&=\$6{,}120
\end{align}$
The initial balance on the Emerald card after the transfer is $\$$ $\boxed{6{,}120}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Switching your credit card balance to one with a low APR can be smart, but watch out for these potential problems.

**Which of these would make a balance transfer a not-so-smart move?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: moving the balance over with no payoff plan
moving to a card with a lower APR
immediately paying the balance off

assessment_items.question_content:
Switching your credit card balance to one with a low APR can be smart, but watch out for these potential problems.

**How can a balance transfer lead to more financial issues?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You continue to spend on your now-empty old card.
You transfer more than once a year.
You immediately pay the entire balance off.

assessment_items.question_content:
Switching your credit card balance to one with a low APR can be smart, but watch out for these potential problems.

**What could make a lower balance transfer not work well for you?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a high transfer fee that cancels most of your savings
a long intro period
a low APR

assessment_items.question_content:
When looking at balance transfer offers, you'll see that some are better deals than others. It's important to know what to watch out for.


Offer | Intro APR | Intro period | Balance transfer fee | Regular APR 
:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
**Card A** | $0\%$ | $6$ months | $3\%$ | $22\%$ 
**Card B** | $2\%$ | $18$ months | $5\%$ | $18\%$
**Card C** | $0\%$ | $12$ months | $3\%$ | $20\%$  

**Which of these credit cards has the best balance transfer offer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: card A
card B
card C
